title: Syrian Dash Tagine
description: Sweet and savory, this tagine features the umami flavors of dashi and a crunchy agave leaf vessel. It's perfect for a low-carb date-night dinner.
instructions: Place the agave leaves on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
In a large pan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the cumin and smoked paprika, stir to combine.
Add the dashi broth, scraping the bottom of the pan to release any browned bits.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and let cook for 10 minutes.
Add the chicken breast to the pan and cook until cooked through, about 10-12 minutes.
Meanwhile, bake the agave leaves in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until crispy.
To assemble the dish, place a cooked agave leaf on a plate, then add the cooked chicken and spoon some of the tagine sauce over the top.
Garnish with pitted dates and serve immediately.
